name: Clam Chowder- from a New England Famous Restaurant
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the salt pork into small dice and render it in a saucepan until rendered. Reserve the cracklings.
2. Add the onion to the rendered fat and cook until golden.
3. Bring water to a boil and add the cubed potatoes. Cook for 10 minutes, then remove the potatoes from the pot.
4. Add the chopped clams and cook for an additional 25 minutes.
5. Combine all ingredients in a large pot: milk, cracklings, cooked potatoes, onion, butter, salt, and pepper. Adjust seasoning to taste.
6. Serve hot in soup bowls with top with crackers.
